creator: khera@kcilink.com
text:
>>>>> "CK" == Chris Knight <chris@aims.com.au> writes:

CK> I've checked this on a freshly installed 4.4 box and a freshly installed 4.5
CK> box with a checkout of the ports tree as at Fri Dec 21 09:30:01 PST 2001
CK> (arrival date of PR). The dependency on X works correctly. Also,
CK> /usr/ports/INDEX currently shows a dependency on X.
CK> If you're still experiencing this problem, then you probably have a local
CK> configuration problem.

It has a *build* dependency on X, not a *run* dependency on X.  

The package also needs a run dependency on X, since you can't use it
without.
